# CG Power Commences Commercial Production at Gujarat OSAT Facility

TECHNOLOGY

Author[Ananya Iyer](https://www.whalesbook.com/authors/ananya-iyer)|Published at:

[Add as a PreferredSource on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=whalesbook.com)

CG Power announced its G1 OSAT facility in Sanand, Gujarat, has begun commercial production. The facility has a peak capacity of 300 million units annually. This marks a significant milestone in the company's ₹7,600 crore semiconductor investment plan.

# CG Power Commences Commercial Production at Gujarat OSAT Facility

CG Power and Industrial Solutions Ltd has started commercial production at its G1 OSAT facility in Sanand, Gujarat.  
The facility has a peak production capacity of 300 million units annually.

Reader Takeaway: Successful operational start de-risks expansion; monitors G2 facility progress.

## What just happened

CG Power's subsidiary, CG Semi, has successfully begun commercial production at its G1 Out-of-Package Semiconductor (OSAT) facility in Sanand, Gujarat. This follows the completion of equipment installation, process stabilization, workforce training, and customer qualification.

## Why this matters

This marks a significant execution milestone for CG Power's ambitious semiconductor manufacturing strategy. It moves the project from the development phase to active revenue generation, validating the company's capabilities in the high-tech OSAT sector and its joint venture partnerships.

## The backstory

CG Power is investing ₹7,600 crore over five years to build two semiconductor facilities, G1 and G2. The G1 facility is now operational, while the G2 facility is under development.

## What changes now

With commercial production underway, CG Power can now focus on ramping up output at the G1 facility and bringing the G2 facility online. This shift from project execution to operational scaling is crucial for realizing the investment's potential.

## Risks to watch

Investors should monitor the pace of production ramp-up at G1 and the development timeline for G2. Execution risks in complex high-tech manufacturing and market demand fluctuations are key considerations.

## Peer comparison

CG Power's entry into the OSAT space with its joint venture partners (Renesas Electronics Corporation and Stars Microelectronics) positions it among global players. Success hinges on efficient operations and technological integration, similar to other semiconductor manufacturing ventures.

## Context metrics (time-bound)

The G1 facility was slated for inauguration in August 2025, but has achieved commercial production earlier, indicating strong project execution.

## What to track next

Investors should closely watch the production ramp-up figures for the G1 facility and updates on the construction and commissioning of the G2 facility.
